ba47c96a36f36642.entry_zero: LOOP-BOUNDARY LEDGER, ENTRY ZERO (WO-013 follow-up item 1). Established FROM SCRATCH on the widened (full-loop) instrument — NOT inherited from the adapter-only ledger via a cross-instrument delta (which is refused, not differenced). This is the active baseline the live gate (which measures the full loop) should reference.

scope.host: fingerprint ba47c96a36f36642 (machine_id a28a45c1cac79570, Python 3.14.6, Windows 11, AMD64)
scope.instrument: full-loop — the WIDENED instrument (WO-013 B): the pinned frame replayed through the REAL LiveTradingLoop (adapter parse+apply+checksum + strategy.decide + risk.check + reason-code emission), the event-loop lag sampler timing total occupancy. This is the SIXTH scope dimension (host/load/source/duration/resolution/INSTRUMENT); a measurement on a different instrument is REFUSED, never differenced (MEAN_CYCLE_BASELINE_INSTRUMENT_MISMATCH). Containment behaviorally proved: evidence/WO-013/containment_bite_proof.txt.
scope.load: replay ~1959 book msgs/min, representative of OBSERVED 60-MINUTE LOAD (WO-008b-B-RERUN)
scope.duration: replay 60.0s establishment (pinned WO-009 source, pinned rate ~1959/min, same host, same protocol)
scope.load_work: per-frame PROCESSING COST characterized by the pinned WO-009 fixture frame shapes (SNAPSHOT_FRAME + UPDATE_MODIFY_LEVEL), INCLUDING WO-017 wire-string retention AND (now, on the widened instrument) the loop's per-MarketState work: strategy.decide, risk.check, reason-code emission. INVALIDATION: deeper ladders, heavier per-level validation, additional per-level storage, or heavier per-frame loop work require re-validation.
scope.resolution: NOISE FLOOR (fifth scope dim) on the WIDENED full-loop instrument (WO-013 item 1/3). Established from 9 runs (108.519/108.521/108.708/108.711/108.717/108.897/108.909/108.912/110.303 ms), MEDIAN 108.717 ms. Within-session core ~0.15-0.2 ms (1 sigma, 8 of 9), with ONE +1.586 ms outlier (110.303). CYCLE OPERATIONAL FLOOR = 2.0 ms — CONSERVATIVE: set ABOVE the largest observed noise excursion (1.586 ms) so noise is never read as signal (a floor set too TIGHT manufactures false signal). PROVISIONAL at n=9; ~20-30 runs would firm the outlier RATE and let the floor be set from a tail percentile. PER-FRAME LIMIT (item 3, DECLARED — corrected by the item-2 containment/calibration proof, NOT the naive arithmetic): mean_cycle = span/actual_samples is a SLEEP-WAKE LAG (starvation/responsiveness) metric, NOT a per-frame CPU meter. Below the ~30.6 ms/frame inter-frame budget the pacer leaves idle slack, so per-frame CPU cost is ATTENUATED ~5x: measured sensitivity ~0.2 ms-cycle per ms-frame (10 ms/frame injected -> only +1.97 ms mean_cycle, BELOW the 2.0 ms floor). EFFECTIVE per-frame DETECTION FLOOR ~= 2.0/0.2 ~= 10 ms/FRAME (not ~0.6 ms). WHAT IS CAUGHT: a per-frame cost approaching SATURATION (~30 ms/frame: 40 ms/frame -> +96.5 ms and the achieved rate drops to 1469/min). WHAT IS NOT: any per-frame cost below ~10 ms/frame reports clean — realistic sub-ms/frame changes are INVISIBLE to this instrument. THE UNCAUGHT CASE LOOKS LIKE: a WO adds 0.3-1 ms/frame, mean_cycle does not move, the gate reports compliance while per-frame throughput cost silently rose. Reviewer-known in advance; a direct per-frame timer would be the fit instrument (reported for a ruling, item-2 finding). STANDING REPORT FORM: SIGNAL/NOISE/RATIO; RATIO<1 => SIGN UNESTABLISHED, keep the entry. Derivations: evidence/WO-013/noise_floor_and_ab_report.txt (A), widened_instrument.txt (B), containment_bite_proof.txt (item 2), and this record.

assessments.event: WO-013 follow-up item 1 — INSTRUMENT identity is the sixth scope dimension. The loop-boundary ledger OPENS here at entry zero (108.717 ms, full-loop). The adapter-only ledger CLOSED and moved to closed_instrument_ledgers (valid for what it measured, never invalidated). A cross-instrument delta is now REFUSED (MEAN_CYCLE_BASELINE_INSTRUMENT_MISMATCH), not differenced. Enumeration of scope dimensions is OPEN (host/load/source/duration/resolution/instrument); every anomalous delta is first interrogated for an undeclared dimension before being read as signal.

closed_instrument_ledgers.close_reason: Instrument boundary widened to 'full-loop' on 2026-07-22 (WO-013 item 1). This adapter-only ledger CLOSES. Its entries REMAIN VALID FOR WHAT THEY MEASURED (the adapter process_raw_frame cycle) — NOT invalidated. It is NEVER differenced against the full-loop instrument; the two cross-instrument comparisons previously drawn in the WO-013-B assessment are annotated as WITHDRAWN below.

closed_instrument_ledgers.derivation: establishment protocol (adapter-only process_raw_frame): 1959 frames at 1959/min over 60s from the WO-009 pinned source; mean_cycle=span/actual. RE-BASELINED under WO-017 (wire-string retention).

rebaseline.pipeline_change: WO-017 wire-string retention: _current_ladder_strings returns the venue's retained WireDecimal.wire; the per-level format(x,'f') render is removed.
rebaseline.attributed_to: wire-string retention replacing per-level fixed-point rendering. Three runs: 107.732/107.923/107.923 ms.
rebaseline.noise_floor_annotation_2026-07-22: Recorded, SIGN NOT ESTABLISHED. Measured across sessions; WO-013's establishment runs demonstrated cross-session variation of comparable magnitude on unchanged code. The honest reading is 'wire-string retention costs approximately nothing', which meets the feasibility bar of ACCEPTABLE cost; the letter-closure of FR-018a(f) never rested on the sign. [SIGNAL 0.963 / NOISE 1.0 / RATIO 0.96 -> inside the floor. Annotated per A.3, never rewritten.]
rebaseline.instrument_floor_annotation_2026-07-22: Further: WO-013's containment sweep measured this instrument's per-frame transfer at ~0.2 ms-cycle per ms-frame, giving an effective detection floor near 10 ms/frame. Wire-string retention's per-frame effect is orders of magnitude below that. The measured variation was therefore not an attenuated signal but noise; the instrument was never capable of resolving this change. This is MORE informative than the original claim: we now know the change moved per-frame cost by less than ~5 ms/frame — bounded far below any operational concern, and known by the instrument's MEASURED floor rather than by hope. 'Costs approximately nothing' remains the honest reading and still satisfies the feasibility bar of ACCEPTABLE cost. [Annotated per WO-013 follow-up item B, RATIFIED; never rewritten.]

superseded.end_reason: Superseded by the WO-017 wire-string-retention re-baseline (0.107923s), same (adapter-only) instrument. Retained, never overwritten.

prior_assessments.pipeline_change: WO-013 follow-up B — instrument widened to the full loop.
prior_assessments.WITHDRAWN_cross_instrument_comparisons: WITHDRAWN per WO-013 item 1 (annotated, not deleted). The B assessment differenced the WIDENED figure against the ADAPTER-ONLY stored 107.923 ('SIGNAL 0.791 / NOISE 1.0 / RATIO 0.79') and compared 'widened core 108.714 vs same-session adapter-only 108.979'. BOTH are CROSS-INSTRUMENT and therefore UNINTERPRETABLE BY CONSTRUCTION — two boundaries, not one measurement with noise. The conclusion they were used to support (loop overhead is small) is instead established WITHOUT cross-instrument differencing: by the containment bite proof (the widened instrument demonstrably encloses and times injected loop work) and by the full-loop ledger standing on its OWN entry-zero baseline (108.717 ms) with its own noise floor. The comparisons are retained here only as a record of the withdrawn reasoning.
